admin.telemetry.title: Anonymized Telemetry
admin.telemetry.description: Anonymously collect sanitized error summaries and runtime metrics to help improve sanblog. No personal information, article content, or visitor data is included. You can preview the complete data below. sanblog is open source; all telemetry code can be viewed, audited, and questioned on GitHub.

admin.upgrade_guide.operation_failed: Operation failed, please try again later
admin.upgrade_guide.wizard_intro: The following guide will help you quickly understand and configure these new features.
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.title: Federation & Interconnect
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.description: Choose whether to enable federation features for your site. These options can be changed anytime in settings.
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.hint: After federation is enabled, the system will automatically generate signing keys. More advanced options can be configured in Settings > Federation. Telemetry details can be viewed or disabled in Settings > Telemetry. All options can be changed anytime in settings.
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.features.federa: When enabled, your site can connect with other blog instances or blog systems supporting federation protocols to exchange friend link requests, article citations, and mention notifications.
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.features.activi: When enabled, users on Fediverse platforms like Mastodon and Misskey can search for and follow your site directly. New articles and notes will be automatically pushed to their timelines.
admin.upgrade_guide.v2_1.features.teleme: Anonymously send sanitized error summaries and basic operational metrics to help the development team find and fix issues faster. We promise: no personal information, article content, or visitor data is collected. You can view the full data to be sent and disable this feature anytime in settings. sanblog is open source — all telemetry-related code can be viewed, audited, and issues filed on GitHub.
